# GoWarmCRM

> The execution application for your GTM team. GoWarmCRM sits on top of Salesforce, HubSpot, Pipedrive, and other CRMs to operationalize sales playbooks, surface deal actions, and close the gap between defined process and rep behavior. It reads behavioral signals from email, calendar, and meeting patterns — not just what reps logged — to tell teams what to do next on every deal, every morning.

GoWarmCRM is built for B2B sales teams running multi-stage pipelines (typically SaaS, professional services, or complex field sales) with deal values above $20K, sales cycles longer than 30 days, and 5–200 reps. Primary buyers are CROs, VPs of Sales, and RevOps leaders.

## Core pages
- [Homepage](https://www.gowarmcrm.com/index.md): Category overview, the execution gap, who it's for
- [How It Works](https://www.gowarmcrm.com/how-it-works.md): Nightly diagnostic engine, action queue, AI-drafted next actions
- [Problems We Solve](https://www.gowarmcrm.com/problems.md): Deal rot, forecast blindness, handoff gaps, expansion blindness, playbook abandonment
- [Pricing](https://www.gowarmcrm.com/pricing.md): Tiers, what's included, comparison vs. building in-house
- [Execution Gap](https://www.gowarmcrm.com/execution-gap.md): Why CRMs track but don't execute, and the structural fix
- [Platform Overview](https://www.gowarmcrm.com/platform-overview.md): All five modules — Prospecting, Sales, CLM, Service, Handover

## By buyer persona
- [For VP Sales](https://www.gowarmcrm.com/for-vp-sales.md): Forecast accuracy, rescued deals, new-AE ramp, tool consolidation at next renewal — what a VP Sales can defend to the CEO, CFO, and board
- [For CEOs](https://www.gowarmcrm.com/for-ceo.md): Revenue predictability as infrastructure, VP Sales dependency reduction, investor-grade metrics (CAC payback, NDR, quota attainment), board narrative shift

## The AI Shift in B2B Selling — five-part thesis
- [Part 1 · The pillar](https://www.gowarmcrm.com/ai-shift.md): The generic thesis for B2B CEOs on why information asymmetry has collapsed, why search never broke the old motion but LLMs did, what the buyer now arrives with, the two things a seller can still uniquely provide, and why the operational response diverges sharply for product and services companies
- [Part 2 · What shifted for product companies](https://www.gowarmcrm.com/ai-shift/product-companies.md): The CEO-level paranoia — will someone deliver my product lighter, AI-enabled, faster? CRM as the canary for broader SaaS disruption, what compliance-as-a-service would do to incumbent moats, and the stickiness-versus-new-product decision that defines the next three years
- [Part 3 · What shifted for services companies](https://www.gowarmcrm.com/ai-shift/services-companies.md): The positioning crisis — people, projects, or outcomes. Why outcome-based pricing is becoming the new floor for growth-stage services firms, and why four operational shifts (account instrumentation, senior-in-delivery, continuous account farming, underwriter-sellers) have to happen in sequence, with pricing last
- [Part 4 · Marketing, sales and distribution in the AI era](https://www.gowarmcrm.com/ai-shift/marketing-sales-motions.md): The operational response — SEO fracturing into AEO, cold email past saturation, paid acquisition economics inverted, and the LLM-ready web as table stakes — with an eight-item audit for whether the firm's first-touch surface is LLM-readable
- [Part 5 · Relationship selling in the AI era — the synthesis](https://www.gowarmcrm.com/ai-shift/relationship-selling.md): Why relationship selling is not dead but being reborn, why the buyer's scarce resource has moved from information to attention, why the best sellers have always done this motion but only now can it scale, and what the integrated picture across product, services, and marketing commits the firm to on Monday morning

## Integrations
- [CRM Integration](https://www.gowarmcrm.com/crm-integration.md): Salesforce, HubSpot, Pipedrive, Zoho, Dynamics
- [Salesforce Integration](https://www.gowarmcrm.com/salesforce-integration.md): Adapter pattern, deterministic ID-based sync, write-back

## Optional
- [Blog: GoWarm Insights](https://www.gowarmcrm.com/blog): 36+ articles on pipeline execution, forecasting, playbook adoption, build-vs-buy decisions
- [GoWarm Skills (early access)](https://www.gowarmcrm.com/skills): Playbook plays as AI-drafted, rep-approved actions; built on the open Agent Skills standard
